info.name: Survey API
info.description: Survey Platform REST API — accounts, surveys (sections, fields, conditional rules, field dependencies), responses, and audit logs.

item.name: Accounts
item.description: User registration, JWT authentication, profile, and admin user management.

request.description:
Register a new user. Public endpoint — no authentication required.

Only admins can create users with roles other than `customer`. Non-admin callers that pass a role other than `customer` will get a 400 error.

Roles: `admin`, `data_analyst`, `data_viewer`, `customer`.
Obtain a JWT access + refresh token pair. The access token is automatically saved to the `access_token` collection variable for use by other requests.
Refresh an expired access token using the refresh token. The new access token is automatically saved.
Retrieve the currently authenticated user's profile. Requires a valid access token.

query.description: Filter by role: admin, data_analyst, data_viewer, customer
Filter by active status: true / false

request.description: List all users. Admin only. Supports filtering by `role` and `is_active`, and free-text `search` across email, first_name, last_name.
Retrieve a single user by ID. Admin only.
Fully update a user. Admin only. All fields are required.
Partially update a user. Admin only. Only include the fields you want to change.

item.name: Surveys
item.description: Survey structure management: surveys, sections, fields, conditional rules, and field dependencies.

item.name: Surveys
item.description: Top-level survey CRUD. Admins have full access; other authenticated users have read-only access. Customers only see published surveys.

query.description: Filter by status: draft, published, archived
Order by: created_at, title (prefix with - for descending)

request.description: List all surveys with optional filtering by status, search, and ordering. Customers only see published surveys.

response.name: 200 — Paginated survey list
response.body:
{
    "count": 2,
    "next": null,
    "previous": null,
    "results": [
        {
            "id": 1,
            "title": "Customer Satisfaction Survey",
            "description": "Help us improve our services",
            "created_by": "admin@example.com (Admin)",
            "status": "draft",
            "created_at": "2026-03-01T10:00:00Z",
            "updated_at": "2026-03-01T10:00:00Z"
        },
        {
            "id": 2,
            "title": "Employee Satisfaction Survey",
            "description": "Annual employee feedback",
            "created_by": "admin@example.com (Admin)",
            "status": "draft",
            "created_at": "2026-02-28T09:00:00Z",
            "updated_at": "2026-02-28T09:00:00Z"
        }
    ]
}

request.description:
Create a new survey. Admin only.

New surveys must be created with `draft` status. Status transitions: draft -> published -> archived.

The `survey_id` collection variable is automatically set from the response.

response.name: 201 — Survey created

request.description:
Retrieve a survey with its full structure: sections, fields, conditional rules, and field dependencies.

Response is cached in Redis for 15 minutes. Non-admin users only see sections that contain fields.

response.name: 200 — Full survey detail
response.body:
{
    "id": 1,
    "title": "Customer Satisfaction Survey",
    "description": "Help us improve our services by sharing your feedback.",
    "created_by": "admin@example.com (Admin)",
    "status": "published",
    "created_at": "2026-03-01T10:00:00Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-03-02T14:30:00Z",
    "sections": [
        {
            "id": 1,
            "survey": 1,
            "title": "Personal Information",

            "order": 1,
            "fields": [
                {
                    "id": 1,
                    "section": 1,
                    "label": "Full Name",
                    "field_type": "text",
                    "required": true,
                    "order": 1,

                    "is_encrypted": false,
                    "validation_rules": {"min_length": 2, "max_length": 100}
                },
                {
                    "id": 2,

                    "label": "Email Address",
                    "field_type": "email",
                    "required": true,
                    "order": 2,
                    "options": [],

                    "validation_rules": {}
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "id": 2,
            "survey": 1,
            "title": "Feedback",
            "description": "Your experience with our service",
            "order": 2,
            "fields": [
                {
                    "id": 3,
                    "section": 2,
                    "label": "Overall Rating",
                    "field_type": "dropdown",

                    "order": 1,

                    "is_encrypted": false,

                },
                {

                    "validation_rules": {"max_length": 1000}
                }
            ]
        }

    "conditional_rules": [
        {
            "id": 1,
            "target_section": 2,
            "target_field": null,
            "depends_on_field": 1,
            "operator": "not_equals",
            "value": ""
        }
    ],
    "field_dependencies": [
        {
            "id": 1,
            "dependent_field": 4,
            "depends_on_field": 3,
            "operator": "equals",
            "value": "Poor",
            "action": "set_value",
            "action_value": "Please tell us what went wrong."
        }
    ]
}

request.description:
Fully update a survey. Admin only.

Status transitions: draft -> published -> archived. Publishing requires at least one section with at least one field.

response.name: 200 — Survey updated

request.description:
Partially update a survey. Admin only. Useful for status transitions.

To publish, the survey must have at least one section with at least one field.

response.name: 200 — Status changed to published

request.description: Delete a survey and all its related data (sections, fields, rules, dependencies, responses). Admin only.

item.name: Sections
item.description: Manage sections within a survey. Admin only. Survey must be in `draft` status for write operations.

request.description: List all sections in a survey, ordered by `order`. Each section includes its nested fields.

response.name: 200 — Section list with fields

request.description:
Create a section in a draft survey. Admin only.

If `order` is omitted, it auto-assigns the next available order number.
Retrieve a single section with its nested fields.

response.name: 200 — Section detail

request.description: Fully update a section. Admin only. Survey must be in draft status.
Partially update a section. Admin only. Survey must be in draft status.
Delete a section and all its fields. Admin only. Survey must be in draft status.

item.name: Fields
item.description:
Manage fields within a section. Admin only. Survey must be in `draft` status for write operations.

Field types: `text`, `number`, `date`, `dropdown`, `checkbox`, `radio`, `textarea`, `email`.

Dropdown, radio, and checkbox fields require `options` (array of strings). Number fields support `min`/`max` validation rules. Text/textarea support `min_length`/`max_length`/`regex`.

request.description: List all fields in a section, ordered by `order`.
Create a text field with min/max length validation. Order is auto-assigned if omitted.
Create a number field with min/max validation rules.
Create a dropdown field. The `options` array is required and must contain at least one value.
Create a checkbox field (multiple selections). The submitted value is an array of selected options.
Create a radio button field (single selection from options).
Create a date field. Values are submitted as `YYYY-MM-DD` strings. Set `is_encrypted: true` to encrypt the stored value.
Create an email field. Values are validated as email addresses. Commonly encrypted.
Create a textarea field for long-form text input.
Retrieve a single field by ID.
Fully update a field. Admin only. Survey must be in draft status.
Partially update a field. Admin only. Survey must be in draft status.
Delete a field. Admin only. Survey must be in draft status.

item.name: Conditional Rules
item.description:
Control visibility of sections or fields based on another field's answer.

Exactly one of `target_section` or `target_field` must be set. All referenced objects must belong to the same survey.

Operators: `equals`, `not_equals`, `contains`, `greater_than`, `less_than`, `in`.

OR logic: if multiple rules target the same section/field, it is shown when ANY rule is satisfied.

request.description: List all conditional rules for a survey.

Create a conditional rule that shows a section when a field's answer equals a value.

Example: Show section 2 only when field 1 answer equals "Yes".

Create a conditional rule that shows a field when another field's answer is greater than a value.

Example: Show field 3 only when field 2 (Age) is greater than 18.

Create a rule using the `in` operator. The `value` is a JSON array. The rule matches if the field's answer is in the array.

Example: Show section only when Country is Kuwait, UAE, or Saudi Arabia.
Retrieve a single conditional rule by ID.
Fully update a conditional rule. Admin only. Survey must be in draft status.
Partially update a conditional rule. Admin only. Survey must be in draft status.
Delete a conditional rule. Admin only. Survey must be in draft status.

item.name: Field Dependencies
item.description:
Cross-field dependencies that modify a field's options or value based on another field's answer.

Actions: `show_options` (filter dropdown/checkbox options), `hide_options` (remove options), `set_value` (set a field's value).

Operators: `equals`, `not_equals`, `contains`, `greater_than`, `less_than`, `in`.

request.description: List all field dependencies for a survey.

Create a dependency that shows specific options on a dropdown/checkbox field when a condition is met.

Example: When Country (field 3) equals "Kuwait", show only Kuwaiti cities in the City dropdown (field 4).

Create a dependency that hides specific options when a condition is met.

Example: When Age Group equals "Under 18", hide age-restricted options.

Create a dependency that sets a field's value when a condition is met.

Example: When Rating (field 3) equals "Poor", pre-fill the Comments field (field 6) with a prompt.
Retrieve a single field dependency by ID.
Fully update a field dependency. Admin only. Survey must be in draft status.
Partially update a field dependency. Admin only. Survey must be in draft status.
Delete a field dependency. Admin only. Survey must be in draft status.

Submit a response to a published survey. Customer role only.

Each user can only submit once per survey. The submission is validated against:
- Conditional rules (fields hidden by rules are not required)
- Required field checks
- Type validation (number, date, email format)
- Validation rules (min, max, min_length, max_length, regex)
- Field dependencies

Values for encrypted fields are automatically encrypted before storage.

response.name: 400 — Validation errors

request.description:
List all responses for a survey. Accessible by Admin, Data Analyst, and Data Viewer roles.

This action is audit-logged.

Retrieve a single response with all field answers. Encrypted fields are transparently decrypted.

Accessible by Admin, Data Analyst, and Data Viewer roles. This action is audit-logged.

response.name: 200 — Full response with answers

query.description: Filter by action: create, update, delete, view
Filter by model: Survey, Section, Field, ConditionalRule, FieldDependency, SurveyResponse

request.description:
List all audit log entries with filtering, search, and ordering. Admin only.

Actions are automatically recorded for all CRUD operations on tracked models (Survey, Section, Field, ConditionalRule, FieldDependency, SurveyResponse) and for response view/export actions.
